---
title: "Cloud Sandboxes for Inspect"
description: "A new package adding managed cloud sandbox providers (Daytona and Modal) to Inspect, so you can run evals at scale without provisioning your own infrastructure."
author: "Alexandra Abbas"
date: "2026-04-29"
---

We're excited to announce [Inspect Sandboxes](https://meridianlabs-ai.github.io/inspect_sandboxes/), a new package that adds managed cloud sandbox providers — [Daytona](https://www.daytona.io/) and [Modal](https://modal.com) — to [Inspect AI](https://inspect.aisi.org.uk/). Each sample runs in a fresh remote sandbox while the Inspect process runs locally; you don't need a Docker daemon on your machine or a cluster of your own.

## Background: sandboxing in Inspect

[Sandboxes](https://inspect.aisi.org.uk/sandboxing.html) are how Inspect runs untrusted tool code — shell commands, Python, file edits, network calls — for each sample in an isolated environment. They're essential for agent evals, where the model is interacting with a real system rather than just emitting text.

Inspect ships with built-in `docker` and `local` providers, and the ecosystem adds three more:

- [`k8s`](https://k8s-sandbox.aisi.org.uk/) — sandboxes on a Kubernetes cluster, with Cilium network policies and gVisor isolation.
- [`ec2`](https://github.com/UKGovernmentBEIS/inspect_ec2_sandbox) — sandboxes on AWS EC2 instances, with VPC, S3, IAM, and AMI plumbing.
- [`proxmox`](https://github.com/UKGovernmentBEIS/inspect_proxmox_sandbox) — VMs on a Proxmox hypervisor, with SDN networking.

These are powerful, but each comes with infrastructure to run yourself: a Kubernetes cluster, an AWS account configured the right way, or a Proxmox host.

## A managed alternative

Over the last year, a wave of dedicated sandbox-provider companies has emerged. The driver is RL: as labs scale RL training, they need tens of thousands of containerized environments running in parallel. SemiAnalysis covers the dynamic in detail in [RL Environments and RL for Science](https://newsletter.semianalysis.com/p/rl-environments-and-rl-for-science), noting that Anthropic is "requiring vendors to adhere to specific sandbox frameworks in most domains" to manage the resulting vendor sprawl.

[Harbor](https://harborframework.com) is one of those frameworks, and we recently shipped [Inspect Harbor](https://meridianlabs.ai/blog/posts/inspect-harbor/) to make 80+ Harbor benchmarks runnable in Inspect. Harbor itself targets Daytona and Modal as execution backends — the same managed sandbox infrastructure that powers RL training environments is what eval workloads need: spin up containers on demand, scale to hundreds or thousands of concurrent samples, no cluster to babysit.

`inspect-sandboxes` plugs that infrastructure directly into Inspect:

- **`daytona`** — runs each sample on a managed Daytona sandbox. Supports single-container and multi-service compose files.
- **`modal`** — runs each sample on a serverless Modal sandbox. Single-container only.

Pick one and point a task at it:

```python
from inspect_ai import Task, eval
from inspect_ai.solver import generate

task = Task(
    dataset=[...],
    solver=[generate()],
    sandbox="modal",  # or "daytona"
)

eval(task)
```

A `Dockerfile` or `compose.yaml` next to your task is auto-detected, or you can pass one explicitly: `sandbox=("daytona", "path/to/compose.yaml")`.

::: {.callout-note title="Multi-service compose on Daytona"}
Some agent benchmarks need more than a single container — a database, a victim service to attack, a verifier endpoint, or a tool runtime alongside the agent. A compose file with two or more services automatically runs in Docker-in-Docker on Daytona, and each service is exposed as a separate `SandboxEnvironment`. For example, [KUMO](https://meridianlabs-ai.github.io/inspect_harbor/registry/kumo_1_0.html) — a multi-step reasoning benchmark available through Inspect Harbor — runs the agent alongside a separate verifier server it submits answers to.
:::

## Getting started

```bash
pip install inspect-sandboxes
```

See the [docs](https://meridianlabs-ai.github.io/inspect_sandboxes/) for full configuration, including GPU selection, resource limits, network policies, and credentials.

[Inspect Evals](https://github.com/UKGovernmentBEIS/inspect_evals) and [Inspect Harbor](https://meridianlabs-ai.github.io/inspect_harbor/) bring a deep catalog of benchmarks; `inspect-sandboxes` brings the managed infrastructure to run them at scale.
